alerts.title: Israel and Iran escalation resumes; mutual ceasefire holds but fragile
alerts.summary: After declaring mutual ceasefire on June 9-10, Israel and Iran have halted further strikes, though both sides maintain elevated readiness. Reuters, AP News, and NPR confirm the pause following Apache helicopter downing and retaliatory US strikes on Iranian targets. Threat level remains CRITICAL (9/10) with Netanyahu having called off major follow-on strike after Trump warning. Risk of renewed cycle remains acute.
alerts.region: Strait of Hormuz, Persian Gulf, Israel, Iran

alerts.title: Iran uranium enrichment confirmed at 60% U-235; 1-2 week breakout window
alerts.summary: Iran's uranium enrichment at 60% U-235 with breakout time estimated at 1-2 weeks per IAEA coordination and Western diplomatic sources (Reuters, Jerusalem Post, CSIS, IranWire). IAEA board passed resolution demanding Iran report uranium stockpile transparency. Concurrent kinetic escalation overlaps with near-term nuclear threshold crossing, creating dual-domain crisis.

alerts.title: U.S. carrier strike groups remain positioned across Persian Gulf and Arabian Sea
alerts.summary: USS Dwight D. Eisenhower deployed to Red Sea, USS Abraham Lincoln to Arabian Sea, with supporting destroyers USS Carney, Mason, and Laboon maintaining presence. USS Florida submarine reported in Persian Gulf. Multiple amphibious assets (USS Bataan) supporting operations. Asset positioning reflects sustained U.S. force projection in response to Iranian escalation cycle.
alerts.region: Red Sea, Arabian Sea, Persian Gulf, Mediterranean

alerts.title: Trump threatens Iran with 'very hard' strikes; oil prices spike on escalation rhetoric
alerts.summary: Trump issued public warning that Iran will 'pay the price' for stalled negotiations and military actions, per ME Spectator Telegram post (~49.5K views, June 10). CNBC reports oil prices rose following Trump's threat rhetoric. Escalation language from U.S. leadership raises risk of renewed kinetic cycle despite current ceasefire.

alerts.title: Israeli strikes in southern Lebanon kill 13-17; proxy escalation continues
alerts.summary: BBC and Reuters report Israeli airstrikes on southern Lebanon killed 13-17 civilians on June 10. Strikes represent continuation of Israel-Hezbollah escalation cycle even as Israel-Iran direct exchange pauses. Regional proxy activity (Hezbollah, Houthis, Iraqi Shiite Militias) remains elevated per global_metrics, indicating broader theater instability.

analysis_note: Israel-Iran mutual ceasefire holds provisionally after tit-for-tat strikes (June 9-10), but Trump escalation rhetoric and Iranian 60% uranium enrichment (1-2 week breakout window) create overlapping kinetic and nuclear crises; regional proxy activity persists with Israeli strikes on Lebanon and heightened Hezbollah/Houthi/PMF status.
